items.headline: Residence Inn guestroom renovations permitted for $2.6M
items.summary: The Residence Inn at 6111 San Ignacio Av is permitted for $2.6 million in guestroom upgrades. The scope suggests a comprehensive refresh of guest accommodations at the hotel.

items.headline: Good Samaritan parking garage permitted at $29.3M
items.summary: A parking structure at 2333 Samaritan Dr received its building permit, with a construction budget of $29.3 million. The project is listed as a balance-to-complete job, suggesting earlier phases are underway.

items.headline: City Council to revoke tobacco retailer permits for two shops
items.summary: The council will hold public hearings to consider revoking the business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop. The grounds and specific details of the revocations are not specified in the agenda.

items.headline: Interim ordinance on tobacco sales scheduled for adoption
items.summary: The council will hold a public hearing to consider adopting a new interim ordinance related to state Senate provisions governing tobacco retail. The specific provisions of the ordinance are not detailed in the agenda.

items.headline: Council to review updates to General Plan health and safety policies
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on July 7 to discuss proposed updates to the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan (covering 2015–2040). This element addresses hazards, emergency preparedness, and public safety standards that shape future development and city operations.
